summary: 11 concrete items surfaced from high-signal HN comment threads.

items.name: DeepSeek 4 Flash
items.source_story_title: DeepSeek 4 Flash local inference engine for Metal
items.summary: Local inference engine for DeepSeek 4 Flash on Metal Macs.
items.why_included: Commenters treated it as a strong, cheap local option and wanted more optimization work.
items.evidence: One commenter said they use DeepSeek V4 Flash all day because it is so cheap; another called the project "so sick" and praised the focused optimization effort.

items.name: Natural Language Autoencoders
items.source_story_title: Natural Language Autoencoders: Turning Claude's Thoughts into Text
items.summary: Anthropic research release that turns model activations back into text.
items.why_included: HN readers praised the interpretability release and the public code/models.
items.evidence: Comments said Anthropic was "going from strength to strength in interpretability" and applauded the public code release for other labs.

items.name: TRUST
items.source_story_title: Show HN: TRUST – Coding Rust like it's 1989
items.summary: Retro Rust IDE for coding like it's 1989.
items.why_included: The thread was unusually warm; commenters loved the polish and nostalgia.
items.evidence: The OP got a direct "I love your project" and multiple commenters said it nailed Turbo Pascal vibes.

items.name: RaTeX
items.source_story_title: RaTeX: KaTeX-compatible LaTeX rendering engine in pure Rust
items.summary: KaTeX-compatible LaTeX renderer written in Rust.
items.why_included: Readers were interested enough to compare it with Tectonic and ask about accessibility.
items.evidence: One commenter said they use Tectonic personally for local PDF generation and "really like the project"; another said they would love to see how RaTeX evolves.

items.name: PySimpleGUI
items.summary: Python GUI toolkit reboot with a paid-license reset.
items.why_included: Longtime users still framed it as the quickest way to make simple desktop UIs.
items.evidence: One commenter said "PySimpleGUI is nice...you can make a button!" and another described paying to keep legacy code working.

items.name: Parley
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Stage CLI – An easier way of reading your AI generated changes locally
items.summary: TUI for reviewing AI-generated code diffs.
items.why_included: A Stage CLI commenter said it solved the same code-review pain.
items.evidence: The comment said they had the same AI-generated code-review issue and wrote Parley for it, then praised Stage's chapter-based review idea.

items.name: tuicr
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Stage CLI – An easier way of reading your AI generated changes locally
items.why_included: Another commenter explicitly recommended it as the native CLI alternative.
items.evidence: In the Stage CLI thread, a commenter wrote "If you do want a native CLI in-terminal for this, try out github.com/agavra/tuicr".

items.name: Helium
items.source_story_title: Chrome removes claim of On-device Al not sending data to Google Servers
items.summary: De-Googled Chromium fork with familiar DevTools.
items.why_included: A browser thread recommended it as a drop-in replacement.
items.evidence: A commenter said it is "a de-googled chrome and has the same devtools."

items.name: Asahi Alarm
items.source_story_title: I switched from Mac to a Lenovo Chromebook
items.summary: Fedora Remix for Apple silicon Macs.
items.why_included: The thread pointed Apple-silicon users to a daily-driveable Linux option.
items.evidence: A maintainer said M1/M2 users can install Asahi Alarm now and daily-drive it, with M3 support coming soon.

items.name: Tectonic
items.source_story_title: RaTeX: KaTeX-compatible LaTeX rendering engine in pure Rust
items.summary: Rust-powered local PDF generator for TeX workflows.
items.why_included: A commenter said it is their personal local PDF tool and praised it.
items.evidence: The commenter said they use Tectonic personally for local PDF generation and "really like the project."

items.name: Gapminder
items.source_story_title: Child marriages plunged when girls stayed in school in Nigeria
items.summary: Hans Rosling's data-literacy foundation and visualization site.
items.why_included: A commenter cited it as a go-to resource for explaining development data.
items.evidence: The comment paired Hans Rosling's foundation Gapminder with his TED talk and called him an excellent lecturer.
